clinical_coherence.notes: 42F South Asian with anxiety, depression, childhood sexual abuse, IBS, and chronic low back pain after MVA. Ongoing contentious divorce with custody dispute as precipitant. Mixed presentation: PNES, functional tremor, bilateral functional weakness. Extensive positive functional signs: bilateral Hoover sign, give-way weakness, midline splitting, tubular visual field equivalent (vibration not felt on skull -- physiologically impossible), convergence spasm, astasia-abasia gait. Prior PNES episode at age 19 adds longitudinal consistency.

tool_output_consistency.notes: EEG captured multiple events with no ictal correlate. MRI normal. Labs normal. Empiric levetiracetam started at outside facility continued without benefit, consistent with non-epileptic events. All outputs concordant.

ground_truth_quality.notes: ICD F44.4 correct. Differential comprehensive. The vibration test on the skull as a positive functional sensory sign is well-documented. Prior PNES episode disclosure adds clinical nuance.

difficulty_calibration.notes: Moderate difficulty well-calibrated. The bilateral presentation, extensive positive signs that require careful elicitation, chronic pain history creating diagnostic anchoring, and the prior undisclosed PNES episode all increase complexity beyond straightforward.

demographic_plausibility.notes: 42F South Asian with childhood abuse history, divorce-related stress, and prior functional episodes is realistic. Medications (sertraline, cyclobenzaprine, lorazepam) are appropriate for her comorbidities. Adds ethnic diversity.

educational_value.notes: Outstanding teaching case. Skull vibration test as positive functional sign, astasia-abasia gait, convergence spasm, prior undisclosed functional episodes, and the complexity of chronic pain + FND overlap. Teaches multiple examination techniques for FND.

strengths: Exceptionally rich neurological examination with multiple positive functional signs
Skull vibration test as a less commonly taught functional sensory sign
Prior PNES history at age 19 adds longitudinal perspective
Ethnic diversity with South Asian patient
